title: When to Take a Pregnancy Test 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: When to Take a Pregnancy Test: Reliable Results
description: Pregnancy test timing calculator: enter your last period to find the earliest reliable day for blood or urine testing. Avoid false negatives.
seoKeywords: how soon can you test for pregnancy
blood test vs urine pregnancy test
when to get beta hcg test
intro: One of the most anxious moments is figuring out **when to take a pregnancy test** so it's reliable. Testing too early can give a **false negative**. This calculator tells you the earliest reliable date for an **at-home urine test** (drugstore) and a **blood test** (quantitative beta-hCG) based on your cycle.
keyTakeaway: A **urine test** is reliable from your **first day of missed period** (~14 DPO). A **blood test** can detect pregnancy as early as **8-10 DPO** (days post-ovulation).
useCases: You had intercourse during your fertile window and want to know when to test.
You want to know if you can trust an at-home drugstore test.
You took an early test that was negative — should you test again?
You want to know when to order a blood beta-hCG test.
You're trying to conceive and want the earliest reliable testing date.

fields.label: First day of your last menstrual period

dataUpdate.notes: Based on sensitivity of commercial pregnancy tests.

example.title: Example: Last period April 1, 28-day cycle
steps: **Urine test**: reliable from ~April 29 (day of missed period).
example.result: **Blood test**: reliable from **April 23**. **Urine test**: reliable from **April 29** (first day of missed period).

explanation:
## How Do Pregnancy Tests Work?

They detect the hormone **hCG (human chorionic gonadotropin)** produced after **implantation** of the embryo (~6-10 days post-ovulation).

| Test Type | Sensitivity | Reliable from |
|---|---|---|
| **Blood** (quantitative beta-hCG) | 5 mIU/mL | 8-10 DPO |
| **Urine** (sensitive) | 10-25 mIU/mL | 12-14 DPO (day of missed period) |
| **Urine** (standard) | 25-50 mIU/mL | 14+ DPO |

## DPO = Days Post-Ovulation

Everything is measured from **ovulation** (not from intercourse):
- **Implantation**: 6-12 DPO (average day 9)
- **hCG detectable in blood**: 8-10 DPO
- **hCG detectable in urine**: 12-14 DPO

## What if your test is negative but your period hasn't arrived?

- **Retest in 3-5 days**: hCG doubles every 48-72 hours.
- If still negative and no period: contact your OB-GYN.
- Possible causes of late period without pregnancy: stress, weight changes, thyroid issues, PCOS.

## Related Calculators

- [Beta-hCG Levels](/beta-hcg) — hCG levels by week.
- [Ovulation Calendar](/ovulation-fertile-days) — your fertile window.
- [Due Date Calculator](/due-date) — if your test is positive.

faq.q: When is the earliest day I can take a pregnancy test?
faq.a: A **blood test** can detect pregnancy as early as **8-10 days** after ovulation. A **home urine test** (drugstore) is reliable starting on **your first day of missed period** (~14 DPO).

faq.q: Is a first morning urine pregnancy test more accurate?
faq.a: Yes, **first morning urine** is more concentrated in hCG. If you test at other times of day, hCG may be diluted and could show a false negative in early pregnancy.

faq.q: Can I take a pregnancy test before a missed period?
faq.a: Some 'early' or 'ultra-sensitive' tests claim detection 4-5 days before your period, but reliability is lower (~60-70%). For accurate results, wait for **your first day of missed period**.

faq.q: What does a faint line on a pregnancy test mean?
faq.a: A faint line is **positive**. Any line, no matter how faint, indicates hCG is present. Retest in 48 hours — the line should be darker if the pregnancy is progressing normally.
Yes, they have **97-99% accuracy** when used correctly (from day of missed period, first morning urine). Cheap brands work just as well as expensive ones.

faq.q: Can you get a false positive on a pregnancy test?
faq.a: Very **rarely**. False positives can happen from: fertility medications containing hCG, chemical pregnancy (early miscarriage), rare hormone-producing tumors, or an expired test.

faq.q: When should I get a blood pregnancy test (beta-hCG)?
faq.a: If you want early confirmation, you can get a blood test **8-10 days after ovulation** (about a week before your missed period). It's more precise than urine tests and gives a numerical value to track hCG rise.

faq.q: What should I do if my test is negative but my period hasn't come?
faq.a: Wait 3-5 days and test again — hCG levels double every 2-3 days. If you're still negative and your period is late, contact your doctor to rule out other causes.
